name: Hawaiian Mission Houses
tagline: The oldest surviving Western buildings in Hawaii (1821) — honest, nuanced storytelling about missionaries' complex legacy.
parking: Metered street parking on King St
bestTime: Guided tour times vary; Wednesday evening events are popular
talkStory: The 1821 Frame House was prefabricated in Boston, shipped around Cape Horn, and is the oldest surviving Western-style structure in Hawaii. Inside the printing house, missionaries and Hawaiian scholars created the 12-letter Hawaiian alphabet and printed the first Hawaiian-language Bible — a project that took 17 years and helped preserve the language. The museum doesn't shy away from the missionaries' role in dismantling Hawaiian cultural practices. Wednesday evening 'Twilight Tours' feature costumed interpreters and candlelight — a totally different vibe from daytime visits.
